items.title: Aljamain Sterling calls for featherweight title shot after UFC 116 win
items.summary: Sterling wants a featherweight title shot against Volkanovski after a unanimous win over Zalal in Las Vegas.

items.title: How American taxpayers fund Trump’s wars
items.summary: Americans worked 50 days in 2025 to fund war. Contractors got twice the tax money that US troops did.

items.title: World reacts to shooting at White House correspondents’ dinner
items.summary: US politicians from across the aisle, world leaders condemn the attack and express relief that Trump is safe.

items.title: Anthony Edwards injures knee in Timberwolves’ Game 4 win against Nuggets
items.summary: Edward's teammate Donte DiVincenzo was also hurt as Minnesota rallied to beat Denver and take a 3-1 playoff series lead.

items.title: We can make sure another Chornobyl disaster does not happen, here is how
items.summary: In war and peace, decentralised renewable energy systems provide a safe and resilient alternative.

items.title: Washington shooting: Is Trump safe? What we know so far
items.summary: The US president is unharmed after being rushed out of the White House correspondents’ dinner in Washington.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 58 as Tehran-Washington talks stall?
items.summary: Trump calls off planned trip to Pakistan by his envoys, in the latest setback to efforts to end the war with Iran.

items.title: Injured Salah has played his last game for Liverpool: Egypt team official
items.summary: Hamstring tear will keep Salah out for four weeks, but he will be fit for Egypt at the World Cup, Ibrahim Hassan says.

items.title: Trump confirms officer shot at White House Correspondents’ gala
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has called a shooter who tried to rush the White House Correspondents’ gala, ‘a sick person.’

items.title: Video: Colombia blames dissident FARC rebel for deadly bus bomb
items.summary: A bomb-laden bus has exploded, killing at least 14 people on Colombia’s Pan-American Highway to Venezuela.

items.title: Trump updates: Suspect arrested after shooting at correspondents’ dinner
items.summary: These were the updates from the shooting at the White House correspondents dinner in Washington, DC, on April 25, 2026.

items.title: Hundreds of Israelis rally as US-Iran peace talks stall
items.summary: Crowds of protesters took to the streets of Tel Aviv on Sunday, amid fears the US-Israeli war on Iran will resume.

items.title: Dramatic scenes as Trump rushed from White House gala
items.summary: US President Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents’ dinner by security after loud bangs.

items.title: Trump unhurt after shots fired at White House correspondents’ dinner
items.summary: US president rushed from Washington, DC, hotel after shots fired outside gala venue.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran rejects talks under siege, Trump cancels envoys’ trip
items.summary: Trump says Iran did not make a satisfactory offer, as Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi departs Pakistan.

items.title: Protesters stage Epstein protest before White House Correspondents’ Dinner
items.summary: Photos of Jeffrey Epstein and US President Trump were projected onto the hotel hosting a gala for White House press.

items.title: Explosion in southwest Colombia kills at least 14, state governor says
items.summary: Authorities in Cauca region demand 'decisive' government action after deadly explosion on Pan-American Highway.

items.title: How serious is the rift in NATO?
items.summary: Trump fury over Europeans' refusal to join Iran war.

items.title: Family longest held in US immigration detention re-arrested after release
items.summary: Lawyers say El Gamal family detained by Trump administration hours after returning home from 10-month detention.

items.title: Trump puts onus on Iran’s authorities as they project hardened stance
items.summary: Some officials say Tehran must not even entertain any more talks on the nuclear issue amid the threats and blockade.

items.title: Police arrest demonstrators at pro-Palestine rally in Berlin
items.summary: Video footage shared on social media shows two women being arrested at a pro-Palestine demonstration in Berlin.

items.title: Protestor depicts Israeli minister Ben Gvir covered in blood in Stockholm
items.summary: Activists organised a protest in solidarity with Palestine on Saturday in Stockholm, Sweden.

items.title: Barcelona on the brink of defending La Liga title after beating Getafe
items.summary: Barcelona move 11 points clear of Real Madrid in the La Liga title race with five games to play.

items.title: Arsenal narrowly beat Newcastle to regain Premier League lead over Man City
items.summary: Nervy Arsenal beat Newcastle 1-0 in the EPL to move three points clear of Manchester City, who hold a game in hand.

items.title: France defends abstention on UN slave trade resolution
items.summary: France justified abstaining on a UN resolution recognising the slave trade as the gravest crime against humanity.
